localizedDescriptions.en: A document expressing all information required to make a request for provision of a specified service by another healthcare provider or organisation.

list.label: Other clinicians to receive copies of referral

localizedLabels.en: Other clinicians to receive copies of referral

localizedDescriptions.en: Personal demographics including name(s) , addresse(s) and telecommunication contact details.
Details of personal name of an individual, provider or third party.
One or more names used by an individual.
Sex of individual is not able to be determined.
To record details of one or more personal addresses.
One or more adresses for an individual.
The structure of a postal address representing the residence of a patient or the physical location of an organisation.  ENV 13606-4:2000 7.11.1.
Personal or organisational telephone, fax, and email details.
A qualifier for a telecom contact, describing its context e.g. 'Work', 'Home'.   ENV 13606 - 4:2000 7.11.19.
One or more telecoms contacts for an individual.
Telecom mechanism e.g. telephone, facsimile, pager etc.  ENV 13606-4:2000 7.11.19
A structure for telephone numbers composed of separately identifiable elements. ENV13606-4:2000 7.11.18
Date/Time that the referral has been authorised or approved.
To demonstrate a design pattern for representation Referral details within a Referral composition.
Request for provision of a specified service by another healthcare provider or organisation.
Identification of the service requested. This is often coded with an external terminology.
A detailed narrative description of the service requested.
A short description of the reason for the request.  This is often coded with an external terminology.
A narrative description explaining the reason for request.
Stated intent of the request by the referrer.
The date and time that the service should be performed or completed.
The latest date that is acceptable for the service to be completed.
True indicates that additional information has been identified and will be forwarded when available eg incomplete pathology test results.
Details of the nature of supplementary information that is to follow e.g name of laboratory results.
The local ID assigned to the order by the healthcare provider or organisation requesting the service. This is also referred to as Placer Order Identifier.
Professional demographics details including name(s) , addresse(s) and telecommunication contact details.
Details of personal name of an individual, provider or third party.
One or more names used by an individual.
Role of a professional in care of an individual.
Personal or organisational telephone, fax, and email details.
A qualifier for a telecom contact, describing its context e.g. 'Work', 'Home'.   ENV 13606 - 4:2000 7.11.19.
One or more telecoms contacts for an individual.

A structure for telephone numbers composed of separately identifiable elements. ENV13606-4:2000 7.11.18
To record details of one or more personal addresses.
One or more adresses for an individual.
The structure of a postal address representing the residence of a patient or the physical location of an organisation.  ENV 13606-4:2000 7.11.1.
The ID assigned to the order by the healthcare provider or organisation receiving the request for service. This is also referred to as Filler Order Identifier.
Professional demographics details including name(s) , addresse(s) and telecommunication contact details.
Details of personal name of an individual, provider or third party.
One or more names used by an individual.
Name currently preferred by this person.
Name previously used by this person.
Name given to this person at birth.
Other name used by this person.
Name used by this persion before marriage.
Personal or organisational telephone, fax, and email details.
A qualifier for a telecom contact, describing its context e.g. 'Work', 'Home'.   ENV 13606 - 4:2000 7.11.19.
One or more telecoms contacts for an individual.
Telecom mechanism e.g. telephone, facsimile, pager etc.  ENV 13606-4:2000 7.11.19
A structure for telephone numbers composed of separately identifiable elements. ENV13606-4:2000 7.11.18
To record details of one or more personal addresses.
One or more adresses for an individual.
The structure of a postal address representing the residence of a patient or the physical location of an organisation.  ENV 13606-4:2000 7.11.1.

The status of the request for service as indicated by the requester. 
Status is used to denote whether this is the initial request, or a follow-up request to change or provide supplementary information.
Personal demographics including name(s) , addresse(s) and telecommunication contact details.
Details of personal name of an individual, provider or third party.
One or more names used by an individual.
Name currently preferred by this person.
Name previously used by this person.
Name given to this person at birth.
Other name used by this person.
Name used by this persion before marriage.
Name in free text unstructured format.
The relationship of this individual to the subject e.g. family member, informal carer.
To record details of one or more personal addresses.
One or more adresses for an individual.
The structure of a postal address representing the residence of a patient or the physical location of an organisation.  ENV 13606-4:2000 7.11.1.
Personal or organisational telephone, fax, and email details.
A qualifier for a telecom contact, describing its context e.g. 'Work', 'Home'.   ENV 13606 - 4:2000 7.11.19.

A structure for telephone numbers composed of separately identifiable elements. ENV13606-4:2000 7.11.18
Length of time the referral is valid.
Duration for which the referral is valid.
If true, referral is for an indefinite period of time.
Suggested design pattern for including a Problem List in a template.
Statements about problems and diagnoses that need to be positively recorded as absent or excluded.
Global statements about the exclusion. This can be used to capture any information that is needed to be explicitly recorded as being absent or excluded within the record.
No significant past history of any problem or diagnosis.

An issue or obstacle which adversely impacts on the physical, mental and/or social well-being of an individual. The definition of a problem is deliberately kept rather loose so as to capture any real or perceived concerns that may adversely affect an individual's wellbeing to any degree.
Date the problem was recognised by a healthcare provider.
The date that the problem resolved or went into remission.
A diagnosis defined by a clinician which is coded in an accepted terminology and may include the stage of the condition and the diagnostic criteria
Date the problem was recognised by clinicians
The date that the problem resolved or went into remission

localizedDescriptions.en: Narrative summary or overview about a patient, specifically from the perspective of a healthcare provider, and with or without associated interpretations.

localizedDescriptions.en: Suggested design pattern for including an Medication Order List in a template.
Statements about medications that need to be positively recorded as absent or excluded.
The statement about the absence or exclusion. Use to capture any information that is needed to be explicitly recorded as being absent or excluded within the record.
An order or instruction created by a clinician which specifies which medication to take, when, for how long etc.
Information about the medication action(s) to be carried out.
The name of the intervention - which may be coded
Dose, frequency, route & instruction as narrative.
Narrative summary or overview about a patient, specifically from the perspective of a healthcare provider, and with or without associated interpretations.
The summary, assessment, conclusions or evaluation of the clinical findings.
Suggested design pattern for including an Adverse Reaction List in a template.
Statements about Adverse Reactions that need to be positively recorded as absent or excluded.
Global statements about the exclusion. This can be used to capture any information that is needed to be explicitly recorded as being absent or excluded within the record.
No known adverse reactions to any substance.
Recording the presence of a harmful or undesirable response to an agent or substance including food, as determined by the clinician - excluding poisoning and abnormal use
The agent or substance or class that caused the adverse reaction.
Details of the reaction experienced by the person.
A description of the reaction to this agent as experienced by the person.

localizedDescriptions.en: Suggested design pattern for including Diagnostic Reports in a template.

To record the result of a laboratory test which may be used to record a single valued test but will often be specialised or templated to represent multiple value or 'panel' tests.
This archetype also acts as the parent for specialisations appropriate for more specific laboratory tests microbiology, histopathology.

Specific identifier for this lab test. e.g. Full blood count , blood glucose, urine microbiology.  May equate to the result name for a single value result. Commonly a coded term e.g from LOINC or SNOMED-CT.

children.name: Laboratory test type

localizedDescriptions.en: The type of high-level diagnostic service e.g. biochemistry, haematology.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.diagnostic_reports.v1 and name/value='Diagnostic Investigations']/items[openEHR-EHR-OBSERVATION.lab_test.v1]/data[at0001]/events[at0002]/data[at0003]/items[at0077 and name/value='Laboratory test type']/value

localizedDescriptions.en: The status of the lab test as a whole.
This is an initial or interim result - further updates are anticipated.
This is the final result. No further alterations are anticipated.
This is a supplementary result in addition to the Interim result.
This is a Correction which should replace any previous results.
An overall interpretative comment on this test.
Representations of the whole test in mutlimedia e.g image, audio, video.

The date and/or time that the result was issued for the recorded 
‘test status’.

annotations.comment:
The date and time related to the results status is
useful for version control and cumulative results for the report.

localizedDescriptions.en: To record the result of an imaging test.
Details of imaging, used in requests for imaging, records of imaging procedures and imaging reports.
Information on the quality of the imaging that may influence reporting.
